content.claim: The removal of top-performing students from ordinary primary schools at ages 10 and 12 creates a potential negative externality (brain drain) on peer composition and remaining school incentives, but this effect has not been quantified empirically.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: Assertion made by debate participants rather than empirical study
No quantified magnitude of effect on test scores, teacher retention, or school resources in sending schools
Plausibility asserted but causal pathway not established
International evidence on peer effects exists but specific application to Hungary's tracks not found in research notes
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
